_header.sign_convention: clv_pct = (closing_prob - taken_prob) / taken_prob * 100 — POSITIVE means the closing implied probability EXCEEDS the taken implied probability, i.e. the market moved to WORSE odds than where you bet, meaning you locked a BETTER price than the close (beat the close).
_header.in_plain_english: For favorites: line moving more negative at close (e.g. -110 → -120) = POSITIVE CLV (close is more expensive). For underdogs: line moving more positive at close (e.g. +150 → +180) = NEGATIVE CLV (close is cheaper). Zero when taken_odds == close_odds.
_header.odds_format_note: american: int moneyline (e.g. -110, +150). decimal: European format (e.g. 1.909 ≈ -110, 2.500 ≈ +150). Higher decimal = lower implied probability.
_header.computation: taken_prob and closing_prob derived via american_to_prob or decimal_to_prob (vig-inclusive). clv_pct sign from (closing_prob - taken_prob).
_header.hand_verification_tool: tests/fixtures/clv_hand_rows.json — every row recomputed from first principles below

rows.note: Favorite moved MORE negative: taken_prob=110/210≈0.52381, close_prob=120/220≈0.54545. (0.54545-0.52381)/0.52381*100≈+4.13%. Close is more expensive; you beat it.
Favorite moved toward even: taken_prob=110/210≈0.52381, close_prob=100/200=0.50000. (0.50000-0.52381)/0.52381*100≈-4.55%. Close is cheaper; you overpaid.
No movement: taken_odds == close_odds. taken_prob=close_prob=110/210≈0.52381. (0.52381-0.52381)/0.52381*100=0.0000. Exact push.

Underdog moved LESS positive at close (became a lesser dog): taken_prob=100/250=0.40000, close_prob=100/220≈0.45455. (0.45455-0.40000)/0.40000*100≈+13.64%. Market said it's more likely; you got a better price.
Underdog moved MORE positive at close (bigger dog): taken_prob=100/250=0.40000, close_prob=100/280≈0.35714. (0.35714-0.40000)/0.40000*100≈-10.71%. Close was cheaper; you overpaid.
Heavy favorite moved more negative: taken_prob=200/300≈0.66667, close_prob=220/320=0.68750. (0.68750-0.66667)/0.66667*100≈+3.13%. You got the heavy fav at a lighter price.
Heavy favorite became less favored: taken_prob=200/300≈0.66667, close_prob=180/280≈0.64286. (0.64286-0.66667)/0.66667*100≈-3.57%. Close has lower implied prob; you overpaid.
Even money (+100) moved to slight underdog (+110): taken_prob=100/200=0.50000, close_prob=100/210≈0.47619. (0.47619-0.50000)/0.50000*100≈-4.76%. Close is cheaper; you overpaid.

Even money (+100) became a favorite (-110): taken_prob=100/200=0.50000, close_prob=110/210≈0.52381. (0.52381-0.50000)/0.50000*100≈+4.76%. Market now says this is the likely side; your even-money ticket was a bargain.
Slight underdog (+110) moved to even money (+100): taken_prob=100/210≈0.47619, close_prob=100/200=0.50000. (0.50000-0.47619)/0.47619*100≈+5.00%. Market caught up to your price.
-130 fav to near-even -105: taken_prob=130/230≈0.56522, close_prob=105/205≈0.51220. (0.51220-0.56522)/0.56522*100≈-9.38%. Market moved away from you; big negative CLV.

Big underdog (+250) moved to smaller dog (+200): taken_prob=100/350≈0.28571, close_prob=100/300≈0.33333. (0.33333-0.28571)/0.28571*100≈+16.67%. Market agreed this side is more likely than you paid for.
-150 to -160 American: taken_prob=150/250=0.60000, close_prob=160/260≈0.61538. (0.61538-0.60000)/0.60000*100≈+2.56%. Small positive; line crept more negative.
Favorite, no movement: taken_odds=close_odds=-120. taken_prob=close_prob=120/220≈0.54545. CLV=0. Exact push.
Decimal 1.909 (≈-110 American) to 1.818 (≈-120): taken_prob=1/1.909≈0.52383, close_prob=1/1.818≈0.55006. Lower decimal at close = higher implied prob = positive CLV ≈+5.00%.
Decimal 2.5 (+150 equiv) to 2.2 (+120 equiv): taken_prob=1/2.5=0.40000, close_prob=1/2.2≈0.45455. Lower decimal at close → higher close prob → +13.64% CLV. Dog became less of a dog.
Decimal 1.5 (heavy fav) to 1.6 (lighter fav): taken_prob=1/1.5≈0.66667, close_prob=1/1.6=0.62500. Higher decimal at close = lower implied prob = negative CLV ≈-6.25%. You overpaid.
Decimal 2.0 (evens) to 2.0 (evens): taken_prob=close_prob=1/2.0=0.50000. CLV=0. Exact push in decimal format.
Decimal 1.8 to 1.9: taken_prob=1/1.8≈0.55556, close_prob=1/1.9≈0.52632. Higher decimal at close = lower implied prob = (0.52632-0.55556)/0.55556*100≈-5.26%. Close is a better price; you overpaid.

Decimal 3.0 (+200 equiv) to 2.5 (+150 equiv): taken_prob=1/3.0≈0.33333, close_prob=1/2.5=0.40000. Lower decimal at close = higher implied prob = (0.40000-0.33333)/0.33333*100≈+20.00%. Market moved strongly toward this outcome.
